tool.name: Genetic Clustering
tool.description: with LEA (sNMF) for VCF or SSR data
tool.command:
mkdir -p outputs &&
#if str($input_format) == "VCF": cp '$input_data' ./input_data.vcf && #set $input_path = './input_data.vcf' #else: cp '$input_data' ./input_data.txt && #set $input_path = './input_data.txt' #end if
Rscript '$__tool_directory__/LEA_snmf.R' --input '$input_path' --format '$input_format' --repetitions '$repetitions' --threshold-q '$threshold_q' #if str($k_mode.k_mode_sel) == "fixed": --k-fixed '$k_mode.k_fixed' #else: --k-min '$k_mode.k_min' --k-max '$k_mode.k_max' --fst-check '$k_mode.fst_check' #end if

param.name: repetitions
param.label: Number of repetitions for each value of K

param.name: threshold_q
param.label: Proportion of ancestry from a cluster required for an individual to be considered pure

param.name: k_fixed
param.label: Select the value of K
param.help: Number of clusters to be used for analysis.

param.name: k_min
param.label: Minimum K
param.help: Lower limit of the range of K tested.

param.name: k_max
param.label: Maximum K
param.help: Upper limit. Increase if the selected K=K max.

param.name: fst_check
param.label: Validate K with Fst differentiation test
param.help: If enabled, the best K from cross-entropy is validated using pairwise Fst (genet.dist, hierfstat) with a permutation test (999 permutations). If Fst between clusters is not significant, K is decremented by 1 and re-tested. Only pure individuals (ancestry proportion >= 80%) are used for this test. Warning: this step can be slow on large datasets.

tool.help:
Usage =====
As input, the user provides genetic data in VCF format for SNPs (Danecek et al. 2011) or in a tabular format for SSRs (see format below).
This tool runs the sNMF algorithm from the R package LEA to estimate individual ancestry proportions and infer population genetic structure. It automatically converts input data to the required .geno format and evaluates a range of ancestral populations (K) from K_min to K_max.
To test the stability of the results, the tool performs a defined number of repetitions for each value of K.
The tool follows the recommended steps for applying the snmf function, and selects the best K using a cross-entropy criterion (i.e. lowest mean = best fit). Optionally, it can compare the consistency of the chosen K using pairwise Fst estimated between LEA clusters. In this step, only "pure" individuals are used to ensure that admixed individuals don't blur the differentiation signals among clusters. The user defines a threshold ancestry proportion above which individuals are considered purebred. We recommend using a threshold value of 0.875, which is optimal for distinguishing purebred individuals from first-generation backcrosses (Guichoux *et al*. 2013). Then to minimise runtime, the minimum pairwise Fst value is tested using the boot.ppfst function of hierfstat package (Goudet *et al*. 2022) which performs bootstrapping over loci of pairwise Fst. If not significant (p≥0.05), K is decreased by 1, the analysis is re-run until the minimum Fst value is no longer significant. If the automatically selected K value is not suitable, the user can choose to set the K value manually.
The tool generates two figures : the cross-entropy plot and the barplot of individuals' assignment probabilities (as in the commonly used STRUCTURE program), as well as a tabular file summarising the ancestry and status (Pure or Admixed) of each individual.
Example files ==============
**SSR Genotype file (tab):**
::
Ind Pop Loc1 Loc2 Loc3 ind1 pop1 120/124 200/202 150/150 ind2 pop1 120/120 202/204 150/152 ind3 pop2 124/126 200/200 152/152
Note : The 'Population' column must be included, but it may contain 'N/A' entries.
**Ancestry summary output table:**
+-------------------------+----------------+--------------+---------+--------+--------+--------+ | Individu | Pop_Assignment | Max_ancestry | Status | Prop_1 | Prop_2 | Prop_3 | +=========================+================+==============+=========+========+========+========+ | GA-2_2021_A11-01-m21_1 | 1 | 0.951 | Pure | 0.9510 | 0.0320 | 0.0170 | +-------------------------+----------------+--------------+---------+--------+--------+--------+ | GA-2_2022_A11-01-m22_1 | 2 | 0.908 | Pure | 0.0648 | 0.9080 | 0.0272 | +-------------------------+----------------+--------------+---------+--------+--------+--------+ | GA-2_2021_A11-04-m21_1 | 1 | 0.621 | Admixed | 0.6210 | 0.1802 | 0.1988 | +-------------------------+----------------+--------------+---------+--------+--------+--------+ | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | +-------------------------+----------------+--------------+---------+--------+--------+--------+
Notes: - Pop_Assignment indicates the cluster with the highest ancestry proportion for each individual. - Status is set to Pure if that proportion exceeds the threshold Q, and Admixed otherwise. - Prop_1, Prop_2, ... are the ancestry proportions estimated by sNMF for each cluster and sum to 1 per row (one column per K).
See also examples of VCF formats in: https://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/VCFv4.5.pdf
